C2-H1.title: GMM Analysis of Cryo-EM OMV Populations Distinguishes Biogenesis Pathways
new_issues_raised: OprF directional prediction may be inverted: OprF-poor regions are more bud-prone, not OprF-rich smallest component
BIC-selected K>=3 does not prove discrete pathways without orthogonal validation
Some descriptors (surface roughness, circularity) confounded by imaging conditions in 2D
C2-H1.recommended_next_step: Run replicate-aware pilot with WT + delta-ompA + one orthogonal perturbation; require proteomics validation before interpreting clusters as pathways; treat OprF prediction as exploratory

C2-H2.title: Power Analysis for Subtomogram Averaging of OMV Budding Intermediates
new_issues_raised: CRITICAL ARITHMETIC ERROR: (50nm/3nm)^3 * (0.1)^-2 = ~4.6x10^5, not 200-500 as stated — four-orders-of-magnitude discrepancy
Schur 2016 precedent is not comparable: viral capsid is rigid and symmetric; OMV budding is a continuous conformational manifold
Budding events may be too rare per cell to yield 200-500 usable particles from 200-500 tomograms
70-170 hour estimate excludes grid screening, FIB prep, and failed grids
C2-H2.combined_recommendation: NEEDS WORK — reframe from closed-form estimate to empirical pilot
C2-H2.experimental_feasibility: LOW as stated; MEDIUM if reframed
C2-H2.recommended_next_step: Conduct bud-frequency census on 50-100 tomograms; build empirical resolution-vs-N curve from pilot; state feasibility conclusion from data, not formula

C2-H3.title: DegP Cryo-ET Difference Mapping Identifies Chaperone Role at OMV Budding Sites
new_issues_raised: Species specificity: P. aeruginosa uses MucD (not DegP) as primary HtrA-family chaperone — if using Pseudomonas, target must be MucD
Background invariance assumption of difference mapping violated by pleiotropic delta-degP effects
DegP cage assembly is substrate-dependent and dynamic — stable cage at budding sites is not guaranteed in vivo
C2-H3.combined_recommendation: PROMISING — verify enrichment first, then cryo-ET
C2-H3.recommended_next_step: Verify DegP (E. coli) or MucD (P. aeruginosa) enrichment in OMV fractions by quantitative proteomics/immunoblot before any cryo-ET campaign; use S210A throughout, not full knockout

C2-H4.title: ML Template Matching Generates Per-OMV Cargo Manifests Without Labels
new_issues_raised: DeePiCt is a supervised 3D CNN, TomoTwin uses learned metric embeddings — neither is a simple CC-threshold template matcher as described
Fixed CC > 0.5 threshold is not a valid experimental design — thresholds are target-, SNR-, and dataset-dependent
Non-injectivity of embedding at 20-30A: OprF and OprD project to identical Fourier signatures at this resolution
Per-OMV copy number for individual OMPs may be too low for reliable detection
Membrane shell signal dominates in small curved vesicles
C2-H4.combined_recommendation: NEEDS RE-SCOPING — redirect to large distinctive complex with knockout control
C2-H4.experimental_feasibility: MEDIUM for re-scoped version; LOW as stated
C2-H4.recommended_next_step: Re-scope to one large structurally distinctive complex (secretin, TonB-dependent receptor, OMP trimer); generate knockout/overexpression control; benchmark with precision-recall on simulated tomograms; only then attempt whole-vesicle cargo classification

cross_hypothesis_synthesis.h1_h4_duality: H1 (macroscopic GMM clusters) and H4 (microscopic cargo multinomials) are dual views of the same biological manifold. If both are true, mutual information between macroscopic cluster assignment and cargo composition should approach the entropy of the cluster assignments.
cross_hypothesis_synthesis.h2_constrains_h3: The noise floor of the H3 difference map is bounded by the SNR and particle count analyzed by H2. If H2 shows insufficient particles for SNR > 1, H3 difference maps will not be statistically significant.
